disclosures.title: Circle USD Coin (USDC) — Reserve Attestation April 2026
disclosures.summary: Monthly agreed-upon procedures attestation by Deloitte & Touche LLP confirming USDC reserve assets equal or exceed USDC in circulation as of month-end. Reserves held in US Treasury Bills, government money market funds, and overnight US Treasury repurchase agreements via Circle Reserve Fund (BlackRock-managed). Conducted under AICPA attestation standards.

Reserves backed 1:1 by USD deposits, short-term US Treasuries, and cash equivalents. Attestation available at paxos.com/pyusd-transparency.

disclosures.title: Paxos Global Dollar (USDG) — Reserve Attestation April 2026
disclosures.summary: Monthly attestation by KPMG LLP (ISCA standards; effective 2026-02-27, replacing Enrome LLP) confirming Global Dollar (USDG) reserve assets equal or exceed USDG tokens in circulation. Issued by Paxos Digital Singapore Pte. Ltd. under MAS Major Payments Institution (MPI) licence (full licence granted 2024-07-01); DBS Bank is the primary banking partner. Reserves held 100% in USD cash deposits and short-term U.S. Treasury bills, segregated and bankruptcy-remote. USDG is the consortium currency of the Global Dollar Network (GDN), counting 130+ institutional partners as of May 2026 (founding members: Robinhood, Kraken, Galaxy, Anchorage, Bullish; subsequent additions include Mastercard, OKX, Gemini, DBS, Worldpay). Yield flows exclusively to GDN consortium members proportional to minting and volume contribution — retail token holders receive no yield. USDG additionally holds MiCA authorisation via Finnish FIN-FSA (effective July 2025). Circulation reached ~$2.75B within 18 months of launch (November 2024).

key_figures.label: Reserve assets
key_figures.value: ≥ USDG circulation (100% USD deposits + short-term T-bills, DBS Bank)

key_figures.label: Yield model
key_figures.value: 100% of reserve income to GDN partners; retail holders receive no yield

disclosures.title: Paxos Dollar (USDP) — Reserve Attestation April 2026
disclosures.summary: Monthly attestation by KPMG LLP (effective 2025-02-28, replacing WithumSmith+Brown uniformly across all Paxos products) confirming Paxos Dollar (USDP) reserve assets equal or exceed USDP tokens in circulation. Issued by Paxos Trust Company, LLC under NYDFS Limited Purpose Trust Charter. Reserves held 100% in cash and cash equivalents: USD deposits at FDIC-insured banks and short-duration U.S. Treasury bills (under 90 days), held in segregated, bankruptcy-remote accounts. USDP circulation has declined significantly from a ~$1B+ peak in 2022–2023 to approximately $32–41M as of mid-2026. Paxos no longer proactively publishes monthly USDP reserve reports; attestations are available on request (press@paxos.com). No publicly accessible static PDF link — document served via JavaScript-rendered transparency page.

disclosures.title: Tether USD (USDT) — ISAE 3000R Assurance Report 31 December 2025
disclosures.summary: ISAE 3000R assurance report prepared by BDO Advisory Services S.r.l. (Milan, Italy) providing a point-in-time opinion on Tether International S.A. de C.V.'s reserves as of 31 December 2025. Issuing entity was redomiciled from BVI to El Salvador in January 2025 and holds a Stablecoin Issuer authorisation from El Salvador's CNAD. Total reserves of $192.9B exceed liabilities of $186.5B (approx. 103.4% collateralised). Reserve composition: US Treasury Bills ($122.3B, 63.4%), overnight and term reverse repurchase agreements ($24.8B, 12.9%), precious metals ($17.5B, 9.0%), secured loans ($17.0B, 8.8%), and Bitcoin ($8.4B, 4.4%). Report commissioned by Tether Global Investments Fund SICAF SA under ISAE 3000 (Revised); BDO does not express an audit opinion on financial statements.

disclosures.title: Tether Gold (XAUT) — ISAE 3000R Assurance Report 31 December 2025
disclosures.summary: ISAE 3000R assurance report by BDO Italia S.p.A. (Milan) confirming Tether Gold (XAUT) reserve assets as of 31 December 2025. Issued by TG Commodities, S.A. de C.V. (El Salvador; redomiciled from BVI January 2025), a separate entity from Tether International within the Tether Group. Each XAUT token is backed 1:1 by one fine troy ounce of LBMA Good Delivery gold bar. Physical gold (~520,089 troy oz, approximately 16.2 metric tons) is stored in segregated vaults in Switzerland (custodians: MKS PAMP and Loomis — MED confidence; verify from PDF cover). XAUT supply grew approximately 107% during H2 2025 (from ~246K to ~520K oz), driven by the gold price rally. Quarterly attestation cadence: Q1 2026 report not yet published as of June 2026. Bar-level transparency: each XAUT token is traceable to a specific gold bar serial number on-chain. Note: verify exact issuing entity name on PDF cover (TG Commodities vs. Tether Holdings S.A. de C.V.).

key_figures.label: Fine troy ounces of gold backing
Peg
key_figures.value: 1:1 — each XAUT = 1 fine troy ounce LBMA Good Delivery gold

disclosures.title: Mountain Protocol USDM — Final Reserve Attestation 23 October 2025
disclosures.summary: Final accountant's report by Nephos Group (UK, ATA-registered; signing accountant: Joseph David) attesting to the reserve composition of Mountain Protocol's USDM stablecoin. USDM was backed 100% by short-duration US Treasury Bills (average duration ≤ 60 days) and distributed daily yield via an on-chain rebasing mechanism (rewardMultiplier), with historical APY tracking US T-Bill rates (approximately 4–5%). Mountain Protocol holds BMA Digital Asset Business Licence No. 202302512 (Bermuda). The protocol initiated a three-phase wind-down in May 2025: yield reduced to 0% from June 12, primary market closed August 22, 2025, and remaining reserves migrated on-chain to a Uniswap AMM pool. This October 2025 report is the final third-party attestation; post–Phase 3 reserve verification is on-chain only. Product is no longer operational.

disclosures.title: BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (BUIDL) — Reg D Form D/A Amendment July 2025
disclosures.summary: Regulation D Form D/A amendment filed with the SEC by BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund Ltd. (BVI-incorporated). Reports cumulative securities sales under Rule 506 exemption (§3(c)(7) qualified purchasers only). Total amount sold: $3.25 billion across 42 investors as of filing date. Note: BUIDL is a Reg D exempt private fund — N-MFP forms do not apply. On-chain AUM approximately $2.42 billion as of May 2026 (net of redemptions, from third-party tracker rwa.xyz).

disclosures.title: Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund (BENJI) — N-MFP Filing Q1 2025
disclosures.summary: SEC Form N-MFP monthly portfolio holdings report for the Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund, a tokenized fund on Stellar and Polygon. Portfolio consists of US government securities and repurchase agreements. Regulatory filing publicly accessible via SEC EDGAR.

disclosures.title: Ondo USD Yield (USDY) — Reserve Attestation Q1 2025
disclosures.summary: Third-party attestation of USDY reserve composition, confirming backing by short-term US Treasury bills and bank demand deposits. Attestation conducted by Withum Smith+Brown. Reserve value confirmed to exceed total USDY supply outstanding.

disclosures.title: BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (BUIDL) — NAV Statement Q1 2025
disclosures.summary: Monthly NAV disclosure for BUIDL, the tokenized money market fund investing in US Treasury bills, cash, and repo agreements. Fund total assets and per-share NAV maintained at $1.00. Distributed across Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Aptos, Arbitrum, Optimism, and Solana.

disclosures.title: PAX Gold (PAXG) — Monthly Attestation March 2025
disclosures.summary: Monthly attestation confirming each PAXG token is backed by one fine troy ounce of a 400 oz London Good Delivery gold bar held in Brink's vaults in London. Total PAXG supply and corresponding physical gold ounces confirmed by Withum. Note: Paxos notified NYDFS that PYUSD attestation switched to KPMG effective 2025-02-28; PAXG's March 2025 report cover remained Withum.

disclosures.title: WisdomTree Short-Term Treasury Digital Fund (WTGXX) — Prospectus Supplement Q1 2025
disclosures.summary: Prospectus supplement for WTGXX, a tokenized short-term US Treasury fund on Stellar blockchain. Supplement covers fund composition, regulatory classification under the Investment Company Act, and operational procedures for token redemption via WisdomTree Prime app.

disclosures.title: Hashnote US Yield Coin (USYC) — Product Structure Disclosure (Post-Circle Acquisition)
disclosures.summary: Hashnote International Short Duration Yield Fund Ltd. (Cayman Islands, CIMA-registered mutual fund, LEI 25490083NU09WKP5Z570) issues USYC tokens representing short-duration U.S. Treasury bill and overnight reverse repo exposure. Circle Internet Group acquired Hashnote Holdings LLC effective January 21, 2025 (~$99.8M); token administration transferred to Circle International Bermuda Limited (CIBL), regulated by the Bermuda Monetary Authority. DRW (Cumberland) remains strategic liquidity partner. Annual fund audit by Valaston International Limited (CIMA-approved specialist Cayman fund auditor). No public attestation PDF is released — transparency is provided via on-chain daily NAV oracle; fund documents available on request to qualified investors only. Eligible investors: non-U.S. persons (Reg S). As of mid-2026, USYC AUM is approximately $2.957B, making it the world’s largest tokenized money market fund. Performance fee: 10% of yield (verify against offering docs).

disclosures.title: HKSAR Digital Green Bond — Final Terms and Settlement Confirmation 2024
disclosures.summary: Final terms for the second tranche of the HKSAR Government tokenized green bond, issued on Goldman Sachs's GS DAP platform. HKD 6 billion issuance, 2-year maturity. First multi-currency tokenized government bond globally, settling in HKD, USD, EUR, and RMB simultaneously via atomic settlement.

disclosures.title: Superstate Short Duration US Government Securities Fund — Initial Reg D Form D Filing 2024
disclosures.summary: Superstate Short Duration US Government Securities Fund is a Delaware statutory trust series (Superstate Asset Trust) issuing USTB tokens under Regulation D Rule 506(c), Section 3(c)(7), restricted to U.S. qualified purchasers. The fund holds exclusively short-duration U.S. Treasury bills and government agency securities (~28-day weighted average maturity, no repos). Annual audits are conducted by Ernst & Young LLP; custodian is UMB Bank N.A. (current product page references BNY Mellon — custodian transition unconfirmed). In March 2026, Superstate announced transfer of investment management to Invesco Advisers, Inc.; upon completion (expected Q2 2026) the fund will be renamed ‘Invesco Short Duration US Government Securities Fund’ while retaining USTB smart contracts. Superstate continues as on-chain technology partner. This entry references the EDGAR Form D (CIK 2004367, 2024 filing). Initial Form D filing date 2024 (exact day TBD; EDGAR access blocked at research time, to be verified manually).

disclosures.title: Ondo OUSG (Ondo I LP) — Initial Reg D Form D Filing January 2023
disclosures.summary: Ondo I LP is a Delaware limited partnership issuing OUSG tokens under Regulation D Rule 506(c), Section 3(c)(7), restricted to U.S. qualified purchasers with a $5,000 minimum. The fund allocates principally to BlackRock’s BUIDL institutional money market fund alongside secondary positions in FedFund (TFDXX), Franklin Templeton, WisdomTree, and Fidelity government vehicles — resulting in layered counterparty exposure across Ondo and BlackRock structures. Daily NAV is calculated by NAV Consulting; off-chain assets are held at Clear Street LLC (qualified custodian) and Coinbase Custody for digital assets. Annual financial audits are performed and delivered to limited partners, but the audit firm is not publicly named. No public attestation PDF or factsheet is released. This entry references the EDGAR Form D filing (CIK 1957431, filed January 11, 2023).

key_figures.label: Primary holding
key_figures.value: BlackRock BUIDL (significant majority); secondary: FedFund (TFDXX), Franklin Templeton, WisdomTree, Fidelity
